name: Fruit Tarts
recipeInstructions:
CHEESE TART SHELLS: Combine the shortening and cheese spread in a medium bowl.
Cut flour into the cheese mixture with two knives until well blended. Shape into
a roll 1 1/4-inch in diameter and 12 inches long.
Wrap completely in waxed paper or plastic wrap.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour (or longer). Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
Remove the dough from the refrigerator and unwrap. Slice into 1/8-inch thick rounds
using 12 (2 3/4-inch) muffin cups or 3-inch tart pans, placing one slice in the
bottom of each. Overlap five slices around the outside of each. Gently press together.
Pierce the bottoms and sides with a fork.
Bake for 18 to 20 minutes in the preheated oven until lightly browned. Cool completely
in the pans on a rack, then gently remove the shells when cold to the touch.
FILLING: Cut each orange section into 3 pieces and set aside.
Drain the pineapple, reserving the syrup. Combine the pineapple syrup, orange juice,
and lemon juice in a saucepan. Stir in the remaining ingredients except the cheddar
cheese, cooking gently over medium heat until thickened and bubbling.
Stir in the orange pieces and pineapple. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before spooning
the chilled filling into the baked tart shells. Sprinkle each tart with about 1 tablespoon
cheddar cheese.
